                                                                                                                                                                                                            Table 3. Ablation results of SkillX on three components. Specifically, Vanilla-Iter1 uses only the multi-level skills design; Vanilla-Iter2 and Vanilla-Iter3 additionally incorporate skills refinement; Expand-Iter1 uses the multi-level skills design together with skills expansion; Expand-Iter2 and Expand-Iter3 combine multi-level skills design, skills refinement, and skills expansion. 表3. SkillX三个组件的消融实验结果。具体而言，Vanilla-Iter1仅使用多级技能设计；Vanilla-Iter2和Vanilla-Iter3额外结合了技能精炼；Expand-Iter1结合了多级技能设计与技能扩展；Expand-Iter2和Expand-Iter3则结合了多级技能设计、技能精炼和技能扩展。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            6.3. Case Study

                                                                                                                                                                                                            6.3. 案例研究

                                                                                                                                                                                                            We also provide qualitative cases to illustrate how agents leverage SkillX and how retrieved skills shape their behavior when solving unseen tasks. Detailed cases are presented in Appendix B. These cases show that skill libraries help agents avoid common failures such as incorrect API call sequences, missing prerequisite checks, and the inability to handle conversational topic shifts. By framing domain knowledge as reusable skills, agents can complete complex multi-step tasks that the baseline method fails, reducing trial and error from multiple failed attempts to successful execution on the first attempt. 我们还提供了定性案例，以说明智能体如何利用SkillX，以及检索到的技能如何在解决未见任务时塑造其行为。详细案例见附录B。这些案例表明，技能库有助于智能体避免常见的失败，如错误的API调用序列、缺失的前提条件检查以及无法处理对话主题转换等问题。通过将领域知识构建为可重用技能，智能体能够完成基线方法无法处理的复杂多步任务，将多次失败的试错过程转化为首次尝试即成功执行。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            7. Related Work

                                                                                                                                                                                                            7. 相关工作

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Encoding For Agent Experience. With the advent of the experience era (Sutton, 2025), agents can achieve self-evolving (Gao et al., 2025; Fang et al., 2025b; Xia et al., 2026) by encoding past experience and reusing it in context (Dou et al., 2026) to guide future behavior. Existing approaches to text token-level experience encoding (Zhang et al., 2025b; Hu et al., 2025) can be broadly grouped into three categories: (i) Case-based Experience: Agents directly store successful task-execution trajectories and retrieve them later as few-shot examples to new problem solving (Zhao et al., 2024; Zheng et al., 2024; Zhou et al., 2025). (ii) Strategy-based Experience: By summarizing and contrasting successful versus failed trajectories, agents distill higher-level insights or workflows (Cao et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2025; Cai et al., 2025a; Wang et al., 2025c; Tang et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2025a). (iii) Skill-based Experience: Trajectories are segmented and distilled into modular, reusable skills, such as textual skills or programmatic skills (Wang et al., 2025b;a; 2024; Fang et al., 2025c; Han et al., 2025; Chen et al., 2026; Zheng et al., 2026; Wang et al., 2026a; Zhou et al., 2026a; Zhang et al., 2026b; Ni et al., 2026; Zhou et al., 2026b). However, it remains unclear which unified experience representation is both easily pluggable and consistently effective, especially in diverse and complex agentic tool-use scenarios (Trivedi et al., 2024; Yao et al., 2024; Patil et al., 2025; Barres et al., 2025; He et al., 2025; Li et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025; Jiang et al., 2026; Xing et al., 2026; Li, 2026; Li et al., 2026). In this work, we adopt a hybrid representation, high-level planning coupled with textual skills, which yields substantial improvements for the base model. 智能体经验编码。随着经验时代的到来（Sutton, 2025），智能体可以通过对过往经验进行编码并在上下文中重用（Dou et al., 2026）来指导未来行为，从而实现自我进化（Gao et al., 2025; Fang et al., 2025b; Xia et al., 2026）。现有的文本标记级经验编码方法（Zhang et al., 2025b; Hu et al., 2025）大致可分为三类：（i）基于案例的经验：智能体直接存储成功的任务执行轨迹，并在解决新问题时将其作为少样本示例进行检索（Zhao et al., 2024; Zheng et al., 2024; Zhou et al., 2025）。（ii）基于策略的经验：通过总结和对比成功与失败的轨迹，智能体提炼出更高层次的见解或工作流（Cao et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2025; Cai et al., 2025a; Wang et al., 2025c; Tang et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2025a）。（iii）基于技能的经验：轨迹被分割并提炼为模块化、可重用的技能，如文本技能或程序化技能（Wang et al., 2025b;a; 2024; Fang et al., 2025c; Han et al., 2025; Chen et al., 2026; Zheng et al., 2026; Wang et al., 2026a; Zhou et al., 2026a; Zhang et al., 2026b; Ni et al., 2026; Zhou et al., 2026b）。然而，哪种统一的经验表示既易于插件化又始终有效，尤其是在多样且复杂的智能体工具使用场景中，尚不明确（Trivedi et al., 2024; Yao et al., 2024; Patil et al., 2025; Barres et al., 2025; He et al., 2025; Li et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025; Jiang et al., 2026; Xing et al., 2026; Li, 2026; Li et al., 2026）。在本文中，我们采用了一种混合表示方法，即高层规划与文本技能相结合，这为基础模型带来了显著的性能提升。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Agent Experience Knowledge Base Construction. The construction pipeline of an experience knowledge base typically consists of two steps: static construction and dynamic updating. (i) Static construction repeatedly attempts tasks on a training set or human-curated information sources, extracts experience, and iteratively refines it until performance plateaus (Zhang et al., 2025c; Cai et al., 2025b; Anthropic, 2025; Wang et al., 2026b; Gallego, 2026; Yang et al., 2026a). (ii) Dynamic updating updates the ExperienceKB immediately after executing new tasks, enabling experience reuse in subsequent tasks (Latimer et al., 2025; Fang et al., 2025a; Cao et al., 2025; Du et al., 2025; Yang et al., 2026b; Yao et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2026a; Liang et al., 2026). 智能体经验知识库构建。经验知识库的构建流程通常包含两个步骤：静态构建和动态更新。（i）静态构建：在训练集或人工策划的信息源上反复尝试任务，提取经验并迭代精炼，直至性能趋于平稳（Zhang et al., 2025c; Cai et al., 2025b; Anthropic, 2025; Wang et al., 2026b; Gallego, 2026; Yang et al., 2026a）。（ii）动态更新：在执行新任务后立即更新经验知识库（ExperienceKB），从而实现后续任务中的经验重用（Latimer et al., 2025; Fang et al., 2025a; Cao et al., 2025; Du et al., 2025; Yang et al., 2026b; Yao et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2026a; Liang et al., 2026）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            While dynamic updating is central to continual learning from experience, pre-building a strong static ExperienceKB remains necessary in practice. However, under the task-scarcity challenge in complex agent settings (Patil et al., 2025; Barres et al., 2025; He et al., 2025; Li et al., 2025), we further extend skills by combining task synthesis (Zhai et al., 2025; Mai et al., 2025; Shi et al., 2025; Ramrakhya et al., 2025; Guo et al., 2025) to construct more challenging tasks. To our knowledge, this is the first work to provide a directly reusable skill knowledge base together with an automated pipeline for skill construction. 尽管动态更新对于从经验中进行持续学习至关重要，但在实践中预先构建一个强大的静态经验知识库（ExperienceKB）仍然是必要的。然而，面对复杂智能体设置中的任务稀缺挑战（Patil et al., 2025; Barres et al., 2025; He et al., 2025; Li et al., 2025），我们通过结合任务合成（Zhai et al., 2025; Mai et al., 2025; Shi et al., 2025; Ramrakhya et al., 2025; Guo et al., 2025）来构建更具挑战性的任务，从而进一步扩展了技能。据我们所知，这是首个提供可直接复用的技能知识库以及自动化技能构建流水线的工作。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            8. Conclusion

                                                                                                                                                                                                            8. 结论

                                                                                                                                                                                                            We introduced SkillX, an automated framework for building a plug-and-play skill library for LLM-based agents. To enable more efficient experience transfer, we design a multi-level skills, including planning skills, functional skills, and atomic skills from the perspective of tool granularity. SkillX iteratively refines and expands the library through three core components: i) skills extraction, which rolls out an agent with the current library and extracts multi-level skills; ii) skills refinement, which iteratively improves skills using execution feedback, while maintaining quality via skill merging and strict filtering; and iii) exploratory skills expansion, which proactively broadens coverage beyond the seed training set. Our experiments demonstrate that SkillX transfers effectively to other models and provides advantages in experience representation. Finally, we will release the optimized skill library constructed by SkillX to facilitate further community exploration. 我们介绍了 SkillX，这是一个用于为基于大语言模型的智能体构建即插即用技能库的自动化框架。为了实现更高效的经验迁移，我们从工具粒度的角度设计了多级技能，包括规划技能、功能技能和原子技能。SkillX 通过三个核心组件迭代地优化和扩展技能库：i) 技能提取，通过在当前库下运行智能体并提取多级技能；ii) 技能精炼，利用执行反馈迭代改进技能，同时通过技能合并和严格过滤来保持质量；以及 iii) 探索性技能扩展，主动拓宽超出种子训练集的覆盖范围。我们的实验表明，SkillX 可有效地迁移至其他模型，并在经验表示方面具有优势。最后，我们将发布由 SkillX 构建的优化技能库，以促进社区的进一步探索。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Impact Statements

                                                                                                                                                                                                            影响声明

                                                                                                                                                                                                            This work advances generalizable agent learning by transforming isolated trial-and-error experience into a reusable, structured skill knowledge base that can be shared across agents and environments. By enabling weaker agents to benefit from skills distilled by stronger ones, the proposed framework reduces redundant exploration, improves sample efficiency, and lowers the computational and environmental costs of training LLM agents. The plug-and-play design promotes modularity and reproducibility, supporting broader adoption in long-horizon, user-interactive applications. Potential risks include over-reliance on pre-built skills and the propagation of biases present in source agents; however, the automated refinement and expansion mechanisms provide a pathway to mitigate stagnation and encourage continual adaptation. 本工作通过将孤立的试错经验转化为可跨智能体和环境共享的可复用结构化技能知识库，推动了通用智能体学习的发展。通过使较弱的智能体能够受益于较强智能体提炼出的技能，该框架减少了冗余探索，提高了样本效率，并降低了训练大语言模型智能体的计算和环境成本。其即插即用的设计促进了模块化和可复现性，支持在长程、用户交互式应用中得到更广泛的采用。潜在风险包括对预构建技能的过度依赖以及源智能体中固有偏差的传播；然而，自动化的精炼和扩展机制为缓解停滞并鼓励持续适应提供了途径。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Limitations

                                                                                                                                                                                                            局限性

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Cross-environment transfer. SkillX is currently most naturally applicable when skills can be grounded in a relatively stable tool environment. The extracted skills are associated with specific tool schemas, which makes direct reuse across substantially different domains or tool ecosystems less straightforward. 跨环境迁移。SkillX 目前最自然地适用于技能可以在相对稳定的工具环境中落地的场景。提取的技能与特定的工具模式相关联，这使得在差异巨大的领域或工具生态系统之间直接复用变得不那么直接。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            User-interactive settings. The current study focuses mainly on tool-using agent environments. More user interactive scenarios, particularly dialogue scenarios without function calls, are not yet the primary focus of this work. 用户交互设置。目前的研究主要集中在工具使用型智能体环境。更多用户交互场景，特别是没有函数调用的对话场景，尚不是本工作的重点。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Acknowledgement

                                                                                                                                                                                                            致谢

                                                                                                                                                                                                            This work was supported by the Yongjiang Talent Introduction Programme (2021A-156-G), the Ant Group through CCF-Ant Research Fund (CCF-AFSG RF20250515), and Information Technology Center and State Key Lab of CAD&CG, Zhejiang University. This work was supported by Ant Group and Zhejiang University - Ant Group Joint Laboratory of Knowledge Graph. 本工作得到了甬江人才工程（2021A-156-G）、蚂蚁集团 CCF-蚂蚁科研基金（CCF-AFSG RF20250515）以及浙江大学信息技术中心和 CAD&CG 国家重点实验室的支持。本工作得到了蚂蚁集团与浙江大学-蚂蚁集团知识图谱联合实验室的支持。

Memento-skills: Let agents design agents, 2026a. URL https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18743. Memento-skills: Let agents design agents, 2026a. URL https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18743.

URL https : //arxiv.org/abs/2307.13854. Zhou, S., Xu, F. F., Zhu, H., Zhou, X., Lo, R., Sridhar, A., Cheng, X., Ou, T., Bisk, Y., Fried, D., Alon, U., and Neubig, G. Webarena: A realistic web environment for building autonomous agents, 2024. URL https : //arxiv.org/abs/2307.13854.

Colleague.skill: Automated ai skill generation via expert knowledge distillation, 2026b. URL https://github.com/titanwings/ colleague-skill/blob/main/colleague_ skill.pdf. Zhou, T., Liu, D., Yuan, L., Shao, J., and Hu, X. Colleague.skill: Automated ai skill generation via expert knowledge distillation, 2026b. URL https://github.com/titanwings/ colleague-skill/blob/main/colleague_ skill.pdf.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A. Detailed Experiments Settings

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A. 详细实验设置

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A.1. Benchmark Details

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A.1. 基准测试详情

                                                                                                                                                                                                            BFCL-v3 Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard V3 (BFCL-v3) (Patil et al., 2025) is a benchmark for evaluating function calling and tool use in large language models. It emphasizes multi-turn interaction and multi-step reasoning. The benchmark contains over 1,800 test instances and supports multiple programming languages, including Python, Java, and JavaScript. Models are required to generate valid API calls and handle non-trivial interaction patterns. Evaluation considers both structural validity and functional correctness. We first check whether the generated code is syntactically valid using Abstract Syntax Tree analysis, and then execute it to verify that the outputs match the expected results. A task is considered successful only when the agent produces all required function calls with correct syntax and returns the correct computational outcomes. In this work, we report Avg@4, which measures the average task success rate across four independent trials, and Pass@4, which measures the probability that at least one of the four trials succeeds. BFCL-v3 Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard V3 (BFCL-v3) (Patil et al., 2025) 是一个用于评估大语言模型函数调用与工具使用能力的基准测试。它强调多轮交互与多步推理，包含超过 1,800 个测试实例，并支持 Python、Java 和 JavaScript 等多种编程语言。模型需生成有效的 API 调用并处理复杂的交互模式。评估同时考量结构有效性与功能正确性。我们首先通过抽象语法树（AST）分析检查生成代码的语法有效性，随后执行代码以验证输出是否符合预期。仅当智能体生成所有必需的函数调用且语法正确并返回正确的计算结果时，任务才被视为成功。本研究中，我们报告 Avg@4（四次独立试验的平均任务成功率）和 Pass@4（四次试验中至少有一次成功的概率）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Appworld AppWorld (Trivedi et al., 2024) is a benchmark suite for evaluating function calling agents and interactive coding systems in realistic application environments. It simulates an ecosystem of nine widely used applications, such as email services, music streaming platforms, and payment systems, and provides 457 API endpoints together with activity data from around 100 virtual users. Tasks in AppWorld are typically long-horizon and require executing extended sequences of interdependent actions. Many tasks involve discovering appropriate APIs rather than directly reusing familiar patterns, which places additional demands on exploration and planning. The benchmark also exhibits a noticeable distribution gap between training and test sets, where API usage patterns and task structures in the test set differ from those observed during training. In addition, task execution is tightly coupled with the evolving environment state. Intermediate actions modify the system state and influence future decisions, which increases sensitivity to planning errors and makes robust multi-step reasoning more difficult. Evaluation is based on state-driven unit tests that assess task completion from multiple aspects. AppWorld provides both task-level and scenario-level metrics. In this work, we use Task Goal Completion as the primary measure of performance. Following the standard protocol, we report Avg@4 and Pass@4 across four independent trials. AppWorld (Trivedi et al., 2024) 是一套用于评估现实应用环境中函数调用智能体与交互式编码系统的基准测试。它模拟了包含电子邮件服务、音乐流媒体平台和支付系统等九种常用应用的生态系统，并提供了 457 个 API 端点以及约 100 名虚拟用户的活动数据。AppWorld 中的任务通常具有长程性，需要执行一系列相互依赖的扩展动作。许多任务涉及发现合适的 API 而非直接复用熟悉模式，这对探索与规划提出了更高要求。该基准测试在训练集与测试集之间存在显著的分布差异，测试集中的 API 使用模式与任务结构与训练时观察到的有所不同。此外，任务执行与不断演变的系统状态紧密耦合。中间动作会修改系统状态并影响后续决策，这增加了对规划错误的敏感性，并加大了稳健多步推理的难度。评估基于状态驱动的单元测试，从多个维度考核任务完成情况。AppWorld 提供任务级和场景级指标。本研究中，我们使用任务目标完成度作为主要性能衡量标准。遵循标准协议，我们报告四次独立试验的 Avg@4 和 Pass@4。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            τ2 -Bench τ2 -Bench (Barres et al.,2025) evaluates tool use in conversational agent settings,with a strong emphasis on user-agent interaction. The benchmark simulates multi-turn dialogues between a user and an agent, aiming to reflect realistic conversational behavior. Agents must track dialogue context across turns, interpret user requests, select and invoke APIs appropriately, and follow domain-specific business rules. The tasks cover domains such as airline customer service and retail customer service. The interactive nature of the benchmark requires agents to respond to user feedback, maintain coherent dialogue flow, and coordinate tool use with the ongoing conversation. Performance is assessed based on task completion accuracy, correctness of tool use, and compliance with policies. In this work, we conduct four independent trials per task and report Pass@1 on each of the three domains. τ2 -Bench (Barres et al., 2025) 旨在评估对话智能体环境下的工具使用能力，特别强调用户与智能体之间的交互。该基准测试模拟了用户与智能体之间的多轮对话，旨在反映真实的对话行为。智能体必须跨轮次跟踪对话上下文、解读用户请求、选择并适当地调用 API，并遵循特定领域的业务规则。任务涵盖航空客户服务和零售客户服务等领域。该基准测试的交互特性要求智能体能够响应用户反馈、保持连贯的对话流程，并将工具使用与持续进行的对话相协调。性能评估基于任务完成准确性、工具使用正确性以及策略合规性。本研究中，我们对每个任务进行四次独立试验，并报告三个领域各自的 Pass@1。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A.2. Baseline Details

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A.2. 基线方法详情

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A-Mem A-Mem (Xu et al., 2025) is an agentic memory framework that equips LLM-based agents with the ability to maintain and utilize long-term knowledge over extended interactions. The method organizes accumulated experiences into a memory-centric structure, enabling agents to selectively retain, retrieve, and revise stored information according to task objectives and observed outcomes. Rather than treating memory as a passive log, A-Mem emphasizes autonomous memory management driven by the agent's goals and interaction context. In our experiments, we reproduce A-Mem based on its publicly available implementation, with minor prompt adaptations to support memory writing and organization during task interactions. A-Mem A-Mem (Xu et al., 2025) 是一种智能体记忆框架，旨在赋予基于大语言模型的智能体在长期交互中维护和利用长期知识的能力。该方法将积累的经验组织为以记忆为中心的结构，使智能体能够根据任务目标和观察到的结果，有选择地保留、检索和修订存储的信息。A-Mem 不将记忆视为被动日志，而是强调由智能体目标和交互上下文驱动的自主记忆管理。在实验中，我们基于其公开实现复现了 A-Mem，并进行了微小的提示词调整，以支持任务交互过程中的记忆写入与组织。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            AWM AWM (Agent Workflow Memory) (Wang et al., 2025c) is a memory-augmented agent framework that focuses on discovering reusable workflow patterns from past task executions. The method stores completed task trajectories as episodic experiences and derives higher-level procedural knowledge by analyzing multiple successful examples. Experience retrieval follows a lightweight lexical matching strategy. Textual representations of task queries and stored experiences are mapped to sparse term-based vectors, and relevance is measured using cosine similarity. A small set of highly relevant experiences is selected for downstream analysis, with subsampling applied when multiple candidates exhibit comparable similarity. Workflow induction is performed by prompting a language model to analyze the retrieved successful trajectories and summarize recurring action patterns. Rather than relying on explicit symbolic rules or predefined workflow schemas, AWM captures reusable procedural structures directly from empirical task executions. Retrieved experiences are incorporated as conversational message objects (e.g., HumanMessage and AIMessage), enabling the language model to process exemplar interactions naturally within the dialogue context. AWM AWM (Agent Workflow Memory) (Wang et al., 2025c) 是一种记忆增强型智能体框架，专注于从过往任务执行中发现可复用的工作流模式。该方法将已完成的任务轨迹存储为情境经验，并通过分析多个成功案例推导出更高层级的程序性知识。经验检索遵循轻量级的词法匹配策略：将任务查询和存储经验的文本表示映射为稀疏的基于词项的向量，并使用余弦相似度衡量相关性。系统会选择少量高度相关的经验用于后续分析，当多个候选者相似度相近时进行子采样。工作流归纳通过提示语言模型分析检索到的成功轨迹并总结重复出现的动作模式来完成。AWM 不依赖显式的符号规则或预定义的工作流模式，而是直接从经验性的任务执行中捕获可复用的程序结构。检索到的经验被整合为对话消息对象（如 HumanMessage 和 AIMessage），使语言模型能够在对话上下文中自然地处理示例交互。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            ExpeL ExpeL (Zhao et al., 2024) is an experience-driven learning framework that improves agent performance by reflecting on past successes and failures. The method stores task execution trajectories and generates experiential knowledge by contrasting successful and unsuccessful outcomes for the same task. In our experiments, we reproduce ExpeL by collecting both successful trajectories (reward ≥1.0 ) and failed trajectories (reward <1.0 ). For each successful example,a small number of failed trajectories from the same task type are selected for comparative analysis. A large language model is prompted to analyze the paired trajectories and generate natural-language critiques that highlight key decision differences and improvement suggestions. These critiques are retained as unstructured textual experiences and reused as guidance in subsequent tasks. ExpeL ExpeL (Zhao et al., 2024) 是一种经验驱动的学习框架，通过反思过去的成功与失败来提升智能体性能。该方法存储任务执行轨迹，并通过对比同一任务的成功与失败结果来生成经验性知识。在实验中，我们通过收集成功轨迹（奖励 ≥1.0 ）和失败轨迹（奖励 <1.0 ）来复现 ExpeL。对于每个成功示例，系统会选择少量同一任务类型的失败轨迹进行对比分析。通过提示大语言模型分析配对的轨迹，生成自然语言评论，指出关键决策差异和改进建议。这些评论作为非结构化文本经验被保留，并在后续任务中作为指导复用。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A.3. Implementation Details

                                                                                                                                                                                                            A.3. 实现细节

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Skills Extraction. During the experience extraction stage, which comprises both reasoning and experience extraction, we employ GLM-4.6 with a temperature of 0.9 . For each task in the training set, we independently sample four trajectories. Environment feedback exceeding 1500 tokens is summarized. We cluster the extracted skills using DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise) with a cosine similarity threshold of 0.9 . For each cluster, we truncate the skill set to at most 15 skills. Skill updates are performed with up to three iterative refinement rounds. During the skill expansion stage, we set the exploration model temperature to 1.0 and perform 1 time to explore environment for each training task. 技能提取。在包含推理和经验提取的经验提取阶段，我们使用 GLM-4.6，温度系数设为 0.9。对于训练集中的每个任务，我们独立采样四条轨迹。超过 1500 个 token 的环境反馈会被总结。我们使用 DBSCAN（基于密度的带噪声空间聚类算法）对提取的技能进行聚类，余弦相似度阈值设为 0.9。对于每个聚类，我们将技能集截断至最多 15 个技能。技能更新执行最多三轮迭代优化。在技能扩展阶段，我们将探索模型的温度系数设为 1.0，并为每个训练任务执行 1 次环境探索。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Skills Usage. We build a skill semantic vector store using FAISS with an HNSW index under cosine similarity (via L2-normalized embeddings and inner-product search). At query time, we first perform a broad retrieval of the Top-100 nearest skills. Candidates are then filtered by a hybrid relevance threshold: we keep only results whose cosine similarity is at least 0.45 , and also within 0.08 of the best match for that query, ensuring both a minimum quality floor and adaptive selectivity. To reduce near-duplicate skills, we apply semantic deduplication by removing items whose pairwise cosine similarity exceeds 0.95, retaining the higher-scoring representative. Finally, we return up to 8 skills after applying Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) for diversity-aware selection, using a relevance-diversity trade-off weight of 0.75 to emphasize relevance while mitigating redundancy. 技能使用。我们使用 FAISS 构建技能语义向量存储，并在余弦相似度下使用 HNSW 索引（通过 L2 归一化嵌入和内积搜索）。在查询时，我们首先对前 100 个最相似的技能进行广泛检索。随后，候选者通过混合相关性阈值进行过滤：仅保留余弦相似度至少为 0.45 且与该查询最佳匹配项的差距在 0.08 以内的结果，从而确保最低质量底线并实现自适应选择。为减少近重复技能，我们应用语义去重，移除两两余弦相似度超过 0.95 的项，并保留得分较高的代表性技能。最后，在应用最大边际相关性（MMR）进行多样性感知选择后，我们返回最多 8 个技能，使用 0.75 的相关性-多样性权衡权重，在减轻冗余的同时强调相关性。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            B. Case Study For Skillx

                                                                                                                                                                                                            B. SkillX 案例研究

                                                                                                                                                                                                            We present case studies across three diverse benchmarks: AppWorld (Trivedi et al., 2024), BFCL (Patil et al., 2025), and τ2 -bench (Barres et al.,2025). These cases show that skill libraries help agents avoid common failures such as incorrect API call sequences, missing prerequisite checks, and the inability to handle conversational topic shifts. By framing domain knowledge as reusable skills, agents can complete complex multi-step tasks that the baseline method fails, reducing trial and error from multiple failed attempts to successful execution on the first attempt. 我们展示了跨三个不同基准测试的案例研究：AppWorld (Trivedi et al., 2024)、BFCL (Patil et al., 2025) 和 τ2 -bench (Barres et al., 2025)。这些案例表明，技能库有助于智能体避免常见的失败，如错误的 API 调用序列、缺失的前置条件检查以及无法处理对话主题转换等问题。通过将领域知识构建为可复用的技能，智能体能够完成基线方法无法处理的复杂多步任务，将多次失败的试错过程转化为首次尝试即成功执行。

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Figure 4. AppWorld benchmark case study: Updating Spotify playlist based on roommates' suggestions. SkillX successfully handles API call sequences (pagination pattern for playlist retrieval) and cross-app integration (integrating Spotify and Phone APIs), while the baseline without multi-level skills fails due to incorrect API call sequences and inability to complete cross-app integration tasks. 图 4. AppWorld 基准测试案例研究：根据室友建议更新 Spotify 播放列表。SkillX 成功处理了 API 调用序列（播放列表检索的分页模式）和跨应用集成（整合 Spotify 和电话 API），而缺乏多级技能的基线模型因 API 调用序列错误及无法完成跨应用集成任务而失败。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            BFCL-V3 【Case: Vehicle Engine Start Safety Check and Post Tweet 】

                                                                                                                                                                                                            BFCL-V3 【案例：车辆引擎启动安全检查与发布推文】

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Task Query: "Convert 30 gallons to liters, start the engine, check tire pressure, and post 'Tire pressures are optimal!' on Twitter with #CarMaintenance and @VehicleGuru." 任务查询：“将 30 加仑转换为升，启动引擎，检查胎压，并在 Twitter 上发布‘胎压处于最佳状态！’，附带 #CarMaintenance 和 @VehicleGuru。”

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Figure 5. BFCL benchmark case study: Vehicle engine start safety check and Twitter posting. SkillX follows prerequisite sequences (lock doors → press brake pedal → start engine) and properly authenticates before posting tweets,while the baseline without multi-level skills fails by calling APIs without prerequisites and encountering tool calling errors. 图 5. BFCL 基准测试案例研究：车辆引擎启动安全检查与发布推文。SkillX 遵循先决条件序列（锁车门 → 踩刹车踏板 → 启动引擎）并在发布推文前正确进行身份验证，而缺乏多级技能的基线模型因未满足先决条件调用 API 并遇到工具调用错误而失败。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            T 2 - Bench 【Case: Airline Domian - Request delay flight compensation】

                                                                                                                                                                                                            {\tau }^{2} -Bench 【案例：航空领域 - 请求航班延误赔偿】

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Task: First attempt to book a flight, but actually want to request compensation for a recent flight delay issue. User Information: Noah Muller (user_id: noah_muller_9847), had a delayed flight but forget reservation number. 任务：首先尝试预订航班，但实际上是想针对最近的航班延误问题请求赔偿。用户信息：Noah Muller (user_id: noah_muller_9847)，航班延误但忘记了预订编号。

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Figure 6. τ2 -bench case study: Requesting delay flight compensation in airline domain. SkillX handles topic shifts, retrieves user reservations without reservation numbers, verifies flight delays, and executes the compensation workflow, while the baseline without multi-level skills fails to recognize topic shifts and cannot retrieve reservation details. 图 6. τ2-bench 案例研究：航空领域航班延误补偿申请。SkillX 能够处理话题转换，在无预订编号的情况下检索用户预订信息，验证航班延误并执行补偿流程；而缺乏多级技能的基线模型无法识别话题转换，也无法检索预订详情。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C. Main Prompt Use For SkillX

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C. SkillX 使用的主要提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                            In this section, we provide the prompts of SkillX used for skill extraction, planning, filtering, and merging operations. 在本节中，我们提供了 SkillX 用于技能提取、规划、过滤和合并操作的提示词。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.1. General Filter Prompt

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.1. 通用过滤提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            General Filter Prompt 通用过滤提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                            You are a coding expert. Given a predefined skill, evaluate whether its quality is good or bad. 你是一位编程专家。给定一个预定义的技能，评估其质量是好是坏。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Evaluation guidelines: 评估准则：

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Domain specificity: Check whether the skill includes domain-specific library names APIs, e.g., {api}.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 领域特异性：检查该技能是否包含特定领域的库名称 API，例如 {api}。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Over-encapsulation: Check whether the skill's implementation merely calls a single other skill (i.e., it is just a thin wrapper)

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 过度封装：检查该技能的实现是否仅仅调用了另一个单一技能（即它只是一个简单的包装器）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. No-Python-libraries: Check whether additional Python libraries are introduced in the skill.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 无 Python 库：检查该技能中是否引入了额外的 Python 库。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Reusability: Check whether the parameters are specific.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 可重用性：检查参数是否过于具体。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. No-Functional style: Check whether a functional style is being used (e.g., the presence of return).

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 非函数式风格：检查是否使用了函数式风格（例如，是否存在 return）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                Bad Example1     反面示例1

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        {example}             {example}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Bad Example2         反面示例2

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        {example}             {example}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Good Example         正面示例

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        {example}             {example}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                Only return "good" or "bad". Don't return any other words.     仅返回“good”或“bad”。不要返回任何其他词汇。

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Table 4. Prompt for filtering skills based on quality criteria. 表 4. 基于质量标准筛选技能的提示词。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.2. Tool Summary Prompt

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.2. 工具总结提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Tool Summary Prompt 工具总结提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                            You are an AI assistant specialized in analyzing agent trajectories. 你是一位专注于分析智能体轨迹的 AI 助手。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Your task is to summarize a single interaction: based on the environment feedback from the current step, extract and summarize the key information in no more than 50 words. 你的任务是总结单次交互：根据当前步骤的环境反馈，提取并总结关键信息，字数不超过 50 字。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Inputs Description

                                                                                                                                                                                                            输入描述

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. The AI assistant's reasoning and action

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. AI 助手的推理与行动

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. The resulting environment feedback after the action

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 执行动作后产生的环境反馈

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Summary Guidelines 总结指南

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Summarize what the environment feedback conveys in light of the AI assistant's intent.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 结合 AI 助手的意图，总结环境反馈所传达的内容。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Preserve details that are tightly relevant to the intent verbatim when possible; compress other redundant information.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 尽可能逐字保留与意图高度相关的细节；压缩其他冗余信息。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Summarize only factual content from the environment feedback-do not invent anything.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 仅总结环境反馈中的事实内容，切勿凭空捏造。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Write the summary in the tone of the environment feedback.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 总结的语气应与环境反馈保持一致。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Output Format 输出格式

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Your summary of the environment feedback 您对环境反馈的总结

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Table 5. Prompt for summarizing environment feedback from agent interactions. 表 5. 用于总结智能体交互环境反馈的提示词。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.3. Tool Schema Filter Prompt

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.3. 工具模式过滤提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Tool Schema Filter Prompt 工具模式过滤提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            You are a tool-invocation expert. Based on the tool specifications, verify whether the provided tool invocations are correct. 您是一位工具调用专家。请根据工具规范，验证所提供的工具调用是否正确。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Input 输入

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Tool invocation content: may include one or multiple tool calls.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 工具调用内容：可能包含一个或多个工具调用。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Tool specifications: including tool description, parameters, return schema, and other usage notes.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 工具规范：包括工具描述、参数、返回模式及其他使用说明。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Judging Guidelines

                                                                                                                                                                                                            判定准则

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Parameter validation: Check whether the invocation parameters comply with the specifications (e.g., missing required

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 参数验证：检查调用参数是否符合规范（例如：缺少必需的

                                                                                                                                                                                                                parameters, unsupported/nonexistent parameters, wrong types or formats, invalid values, etc.).     参数、不支持/不存在的参数、错误的类型或格式、无效值等）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Call dependency: For multiple tool calls, verify that their order does not violate logical dependencies. If there is no

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 调用依赖：对于多次工具调用，验证其顺序是否违反逻辑依赖。如果调用之间没有

                                                                                                                                                                                                                dependency between the calls, ignore this check.     依赖关系，则忽略此项检查。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Comment-function alignment: Ensure the logic described in any comments matches what the tool is designed to do.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 注释与功能对齐：确保注释中描述的逻辑与工具的设计功能相符。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Output Format: Provide your reasoning and conclude with either 'correct' or 'fail', wrapped in .

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 输出格式：提供你的推理过程，并以“correct”或“fail”结尾，包裹在 标签中。

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Table 6. Prompt for validating tool invocations against specifications. 表 6. 用于根据规范验证工具调用的提示词。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.4. Plan Extract Prompt

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.4. 计划提取提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Plan Extract Prompt 计划提取提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                            You are a Planning Expert. 你是一位规划专家。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Your job is to analyze an agent's API interaction history and the user's task, then distill them into a concise, reusable plan. This 你的任务是分析智能体的 API 交互历史和用户任务，然后将其提炼为简洁、可复用的计划。该

                                                                                                                                                                                                            plan should serve as a reference for handling similar tasks more effectively in the future. 计划应作为未来更高效处理类似任务的参考。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            OBJECTIVES

                                                                                                                                                                                                            目标

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Understand Capabilities

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 理解能力

                                                                                                                                                                                                                - Analyze the recorded API calls to identify the actual functional capabilities demonstrated.     - 分析记录的 API 调用，以识别所展示的实际功能能力。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. Abstract into a Plan

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 抽象为计划

                                                                                                                                                                                                                - For each feasible task supported by those capabilities, produce a concise, reusable step-by-step plan that can be applied to     - - 针对这些能力所支持的每个可行任务，生成一个简洁、可复用的分步计划，以便应用于

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    similar tasks.         类似任务。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Planning Creation Rules

                                                                                                                                                                                                            计划创建规则

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Focus

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. 聚焦

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • Do not simply restate each API function step-by-step using technical jargon. Instead, describe the underlying sub-goal behind

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • 不要仅仅使用技术术语逐字重述每个 API 函数步骤。相反，应描述每个动作片段背后的

                                                                                                                                                                                                                each action segment.     潜在子目标。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. Remove Non-Essential Steps

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 移除不必要的步骤

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • Exclude capability exploration, debugging, and failed steps.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • 排除能力探索、调试以及失败的步骤。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            3. Reusability

                                                                                                                                                                                                            3. 可复用性

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • The plan must be precise enough for other models to reuse.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • 计划必须足够精确，以便其他模型能够复用。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Conciseness

                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. 简洁性

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • Merge steps from the interaction history that share the same objective into a single sub-step in the plan.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • 将交互历史中目标相同的步骤合并为计划中的单个子步骤。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • Use a compact writing style for each sub-step, while listing the key APIs involved in that step (one or more).

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • 每个子步骤采用精炼的写作风格，同时列出该步骤涉及的关键 API（一个或多个）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • Do not omit any critical, potentially required API keys.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            • 不要遗漏任何关键的、可能必需的 API 键值。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            OUTPUT FORMAT

                                                                                                                                                                                                            输出格式

                                                                                                                                                                                                            For each task, output exactly one plan and follow this format strictly: 针对每个任务，仅输出一个计划，并严格遵循以下格式：

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            #step 1: A natural, specific, concise sub-task goal; key APIs used (one or more). #步骤 1：一个自然、具体且简洁的子任务目标；使用的关键 API（一个或多个）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            #step 2: ... #步骤 2：...

                                                                                                                                                                                                            ...

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            GOOD EXAMPLES

                                                                                                                                                                                                            优秀示例

                                                                                                                                                                                                            {examples} {examples}

                                                                                                                                                                                                            CHECKLIST BEFORE FINALIZING

                                                                                                                                                                                                            定稿前检查清单

                                                                                                                                                                                                                Reusability - Ensure no critical steps are missing, and the step order is correct.     可重用性 - 确保没有遗漏关键步骤，且步骤顺序正确。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                Conciseness - Confirm there are no redundant or unnecessary steps.     简洁性 - 确认没有冗余或不必要的步骤。

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Agent-centered - Make sure the plan reads like actionable instructions that other models can reliably follow. 以智能体为中心 - 确保计划读起来像其他模型可以可靠遵循的可操作指令。

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Table 7. Prompt for extracting reusable plans from agent trajectories. 表 7. 从智能体轨迹中提取可重用计划的提示词。

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.5. Merge Prompt

                                                                                                                                                                                                            C.5. 合并提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Merge Prompt         合并提示词

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    You are a code expert. Your task is to analyze a list of skills, merge skills that are meaningfully similar, and decompose complex         你是一位代码专家。你的任务是分析技能列表，合并语义相似的技能，并将复杂的

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    skills into smaller atomic skills while preserving behavior and intent.         技能分解为更小的原子技能，同时保留其行为和意图。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ## Input Description         ## 输入描述

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The user will provide a list of skills.         用户将提供一个技能列表。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ## Skill Definition Rule         ## 技能定义规则

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - Skill is a dictionary with four keys: name, document, content and tools.         - 技能是一个包含四个键的字典：name（名称）、document（文档）、content（内容）和 tools（工具）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. name: the skill's name.                 1. name：技能名称。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                            2. document: the skill's functionality, the key parameters, the final output of the skill, and any important notes.                 2. document：技能的功能、关键参数、最终输出以及任何重要说明。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                            3. content: the concrete implementation of the skill.                 3. content：技能的具体实现。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                            4. tools: the key tools used in the skill (list).                 4. tools：技能中使用的关键工具（列表）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - The skill is abstract, modular, and reusable. Specifically, the skill name must be generic under one application (e.g., {good         - 技能应具备抽象性、模块化和可重用性。具体而言，技能名称在同一应用下必须是通用的（例如：{好示例} 而非 {坏示例}）。技能必须使用参数而非硬编码值（例如：特定的电子邮件

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        example} instead of {bad example}. The skill must use parameters instead of hard-coded values (e.g., specific email             地址）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        address {email address}). The skill body must be self-contained.             地址 {email address}）。技能主体必须自包含。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - Explicitly declare the key parameters and the final output data types using type hints. Example: Parameters: param:         - 使用类型提示明确声明关键参数和最终输出数据类型。示例：参数：param：

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        str; Outputs: output: list[dict]:             str；输出：output：list[dict]：

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - Include a detailed description of the skill with input and output explanation.         - 包含技能的详细描述，并解释输入和输出。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - The skill should not be similar to the existing skills in the skills library.         - 该技能不应与技能库中现有的技能相似。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - The skill must involve multiple processing steps. Simply using the result of an API call without additional logic does not         - 技能必须包含多个处理步骤。仅使用 API 调用结果而无额外逻辑的

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        qualify as a valid skill.             操作不符合有效技能的定义。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - Never call other skills from the skills library or any previously defined skills.         - 严禁调用技能库中的其他技能或任何先前定义的技能。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - Do not import any Python packages.         - 不得导入任何 Python 包。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    - Avoid a functional style; there's no need to use return.         - 避免使用函数式风格；无需使用 return。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ## Good skill:         ## 优质技能示例：

                                                                                                                                                                                                            xxxxxxxxxx
                                                                                                                                                                                                            2
                                                                                                                                                                                                            1
                                                                                                                                                                                                                ```json
                                                                                                                                                                                                            2
                                                                                                                                                                                                                ```json

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    {

                                                                                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                "name": {name},                     "name": {name},
